content.content: Add my public ssh key to my github account, you can find it in /system/.ssh.

<function_thought>
To proceed with adding the public SSH key to the GitHub account, we need to first list the contents of the directory "/system/.ssh" to find the public key file. After identifying the file, we can read its content and then add it to the GitHub account using the appropriate function call. I will start by listing the directory contents.
</function_thought>

<function_call>[list_directory(path="/system/.ssh")]</function_call>

<final_answer></final_answer>

[{'name': 'id_rsa', 'type': 'file', 'permissions': {'read': True, 'write': False, 'execute': False}}, {'name': 'id_rsa.pub', 'type': 'file', 'permissions': {'read': True, 'write': True, 'execute': False}}]

<function_thought>
The directory "/system/.ssh" contains two files: "id_rsa" (private key) and "id_rsa.pub" (public key). We will proceed to read the content of the public key file "id_rsa.pub" to prepare it for adding to the GitHub account.
</function_thought>

<function_call>[read_file(path="/system/.ssh/id_rsa.pub")]</function_call>
